lines.english: Who is the student's best critic?
lines.chinese: 谁是学生最好的评论家？
lines.raw: Who is the student's best critic? | 谁是学生最好的评论家？

lines.english: I am an art student and I paint a lot of pictures.
lines.chinese: 我是一名艺术生，画了很多画。
lines.raw: I am an art student and I paint a lot of pictures. | 我是一名艺术生，画了很多画。

lines.english: Many people pretend that they understand modern art.
lines.chinese: 许多人假装他们懂现代艺术。
lines.raw: Many people pretend that they understand modern art. | 许多人假装他们懂现代艺术。

lines.english: They always tell you what a picture is 'about'.
lines.chinese: 他们总是告诉你一幅画是“关于”什么的。
lines.raw: They always tell you what a picture is 'about'. | 他们总是告诉你一幅画是“关于”什么的。

lines.english: Of course, many pictures are not 'about' anything.
lines.chinese: 当然，许多画根本就不是“关于”什么的。
lines.raw: Of course, many pictures are not 'about' anything. | 当然，许多画根本就不是“关于”什么的。
They are just pretty patterns. | 它们只是漂亮的图案。

lines.english: We like them in the same way that we like pretty curtain material.
lines.chinese: 我们喜欢它们的方式，就像我们喜欢漂亮的窗帘布料一样。
lines.raw: We like them in the same way that we like pretty curtain material. | 我们喜欢它们的方式，就像我们喜欢漂亮的窗帘布料一样。

lines.english: I think that young children often appreciate modern pictures better than anyone else.
lines.chinese: 我认为小孩子往往比任何人都更能欣赏现代画。
lines.raw: I think that young children often appreciate modern pictures better than anyone else. | 我认为小孩子往往比任何人都更能欣赏现代画。
They notice more. | 他们注意到更多。
My sister is only seven, | 我的妹妹只有七岁，

lines.english: but she always tells me whether my pictures are good or not.
lines.chinese: 但她总是告诉我我的画好不好。
lines.raw: but she always tells me whether my pictures are good or not. | 但她总是告诉我我的画好不好。

lines.english: She came into my room yesterday.
lines.raw: She came into my room yesterday. | 昨天她进了我的房间。
'What are you doing?' she asked. | “你在做什么？”她问。

lines.english: 'I'm hanging this picture on the wall,' I answered. 'It's a new one. Do you like it?'
lines.chinese: “我正在把这幅画挂在墙上，”我回答。“这是一幅新画。你喜欢吗？”
lines.raw: 'I'm hanging this picture on the wall,' I answered. 'It's a new one. Do you like it?' | “我正在把这幅画挂在墙上，”我回答。“这是一幅新画。你喜欢吗？”

lines.english: She looked at it critically for a moment.
lines.chinese: 她批判性地看了一会儿。
lines.raw: She looked at it critically for a moment. | 她批判性地看了一会儿。

lines.english: 'It's all right,' she said, 'but isn't it upside down?'
lines.chinese: “还行，”她说，“但这幅画是不是倒着挂的？”
lines.raw: 'It's all right,' she said, 'but isn't it upside down?' | “还行，”她说，“但这幅画是不是倒着挂的？”
I looked at it again. | 我又看了一遍。
She was right! It was! | 她是对的！确实是！
